message:
feat(multipart): propagate spool-backed file parts through MultipartForm without re-materializing bytes

Previously, streamed parts that were spooled to disk during incremental
parsing had their bytes eagerly read back into memory when converted to
Part structs via into_part(). This defeated the purpose of spooling for
large uploads — the data was written to a temp file and then immediately
read back into a Vec<u8>.

Now Part carries optional spooled_path and spooled_len fields so that
spool-backed file data can remain on disk through the MultipartForm
accessor path. Key changes:

- StreamingPartState::into_part() no longer reads/deletes the temp file;
  instead it sets spooled_path on the returned Part and leaves data empty
- Part gains is_spooled(), spooled_path(), and bytes() accessors that
  transparently handle both in-memory and spool-backed storage
- UploadFile::from_part_with_spool_threshold() detects pre-spooled parts
  and reuses their temp file path directly instead of re-spooling
- MultipartForm::upload_from_borrowed_part() uses Part::bytes() to
  materialize data only when needed (borrow-based accessors like
  get_file/files/files_by_name), while move-based APIs (take_file,
  into_files) can transfer spool backing without copying
- MultipartForm no longer derives Clone (Part is no longer Clone due to
  temp file ownership semantics)
- Test Part literals updated with the new spooled_path/spooled_len fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
